summary: Best week yet — 5 conversions at $56.24 CPA, well under the $80 target. The -50% mobile bid adjustment we made last week is clearly working: mobile spend dropped from 37% to 13% of total while desktop delivers 100% of conversions. Search terms are completely clean with zero spam. Campaign is now budget-limited, meaning Google could spend more profitably if given room.

metrics.label: Conversions
metrics.note: Up from 1 last week — strong rebound

metrics.label: Cost per Conversion
metrics.note: Best weekly CPA — well under $80 target

funnel.title: Ad-to-Booking Funnel
funnel.notes: All 5 conversions came from desktop. Search terms are 100% relevant — no spam or irrelevant clicks this week.

findings.title: Mobile Bid Adjustment Is Working
findings.description: After increasing the mobile bid reduction to -50% last week, mobile's share of spend dropped from 37% to 13%. Desktop now gets 87% of budget and is delivering all 5 conversions. This single change appears to be the biggest driver of this week's improvement.

findings.title: Search Terms Are Completely Clean
findings.description: Every search term this week is relevant — mobile bartending, bar catering, event beverage services, and Denver-area queries. No spam, no irrelevant clicks. Competitor brand searches (moonlight bartending, wandering wagon, etc.) also show strong buyer intent.

findings.title: Campaign Is Budget-Limited — A Good Problem
findings.description: Google is flagging the campaign as 'limited by budget,' meaning it could serve more impressions profitably. With CPA at $56.24 (well under the $80 target), there's room to increase daily budget and capture more leads.

findings.title: Keyword Concentration Remains
findings.description: 'Bartenders for hire' still drives 89% of spend ($249 of $281). It's converting well, but if a competitor starts aggressively bidding on this term, CPA could spike quickly.

actions.title: Monitored -50% Mobile Bid Adjustment
actions.description: Confirmed the change is working as intended — mobile cost share dropped from 37% to 13% with no negative impact on conversion volume. Desktop conversions jumped from 1 to 5.

actions.title: Reviewed Search Terms for Spam
actions.description: Audited all search terms for Mar 20-26. Zero spam or irrelevant clicks found. All terms are mobile bartending, bar catering, or event beverage related.

nextSteps: Consider increasing daily budget from $40 — campaign is budget-limited and CPA is strong at $56.24
Evaluate whether to raise Target CPA back to $100 to give the algorithm more room, or keep at $80 since it's performing well this week
Continue monitoring keyword concentration risk on 'bartenders for hire'
Track demographic trend (female, 35-54) for potential audience targeting refinements
